songs.title: MONO CONTACT — SPES-R (Functional Minimal Techno)
songs.tags:
[STYLE / CONTEXT:]
[Functional Minimal Techno with a raw, clinical, and restrained sensual undertone.
Sensuality must be purely physical and implicit, emerging only from friction, proximity, repetition, and pressure.
No emotion, no expression, no narrative intent.
Sound should feel close, dry, intimate, and mechanical, like bodies moving in a confined dark space.
Designed as a professional DJ tool for underground clubs and long endurance sets.
Minimal without emptiness, sexual without softness, controlled without drama.]

songs.title: Poderoso Deus
songs.tags: Modern worship pop-soul ballad inspired by Lauren D aigle, featuring soulful, expressive female vocal, piano, acoustic guitar, gentle ambient pads, and soft organic drums. Portuguese lyrics express grace, surrender, humility, and faith, with each line beginning with an English emotion/rhythm tag. The arrangement is simple, heartfelt, and congregational, focusing on sincerity, vulnerability, and worshipful devotion.

songs.title: Ninguém Explica Deus
songs.tags: Female vocal, Bonnie Tyler style: raspy, powerful, emotional, medium-low tone, strong vibrato, British accent. '80s pop rock ballad, dramatic and passionate. Piano and synth pads in verses, electric guitar and drums in chorus, big dynamic build, live studio feel, natural reverb, expressive voice breaks, intentional light distortion, not over-polished. Variable tempo: slow in verses, explosive in chorus. Influence: Bonnie Tyler, Heart, Tina Turner. NO autotune, NO synthetic drums, NO bland performance.

No autotune, no pop runs, no gospel choir, no electronic beats—only soulful, pure worship.

songs.title: Respira em Mim
songs.tags: Modern worship pop-soul ballad, Lauren D aigle style: soulful, expressive, confessional female vocal; warm timbre, honest delivery, slight rasp. Piano, pads, guitar, drums, soft organ, strings. Portuguese lyrics: vulnerability, trust, devotion, poetic lines, easy to sing along. Each line with English emotion/rhythm tag. No autotune, no pop runs, no gospel choir—only pure, heartfelt worship.

songs.title: Nothing Else Matters (Modern Rewrite)
songs.tags: The OME Cinematic Power Ballad Rock remix opens with clean stereo-panned electric guitar and a centered bowed cello, joined by an analog synth drone and subtle ambient reversed FX. Delayed snares and a slow, pulsing bass set the reflective tempo. Vocals evolve from whisper to passionate belts, with harmonies widening the stereo field. The chorus expands spatially with delayed vocal responses and soft choirs in the rear stereo, while a melodic solo electric guitar weaves through the mix. Each instrument occupies its own precise layer, with dynamic automation creating immersive, emotional waves.

songs.title: Não Temas, Eu Sou Contigo
songs.tags: Female vocal, Bonnie Tyler style: raspy, powerful, emotional, medium-low tone, strong vibrato, British accent. '80s pop rock ballad, dramatic and passionate. Piano and synth pads in verses, electric guitar and drums in chorus, big dynamic build, live studio feel, natural reverb, expressive voice breaks, intentional light distortion, not over-polished. Variable tempo: slow in verses, explosive in chorus. Influence: Bonnie Tyler, Heart, Tina Turner. NO autotune, NO synthetic drums, NO bland performance.

songs.title: Tudo Que Faltou Dizer (Remix)
songs.tags: Emotional romantic duet ballad blending Brazilian sertanejo and classic rock influences. Male voice is raspy, intimate, and fragile like B ryan Adams or Ze zé Di Camargo. Female voice is melodic, strong yet broken, similar to Bonnie Tyler or Roberta Miranda. Tempo: 68 BPM. Instrumentation includes soft piano, acoustic guitar, clean electric guitar, subtle accordion, and organic drums. Vocals are expressive with audible breath, slow phrasing, natural vibrato, and emotional timing. Harmonized choruses with distinct timbres. The song structure is cinematic, with storytelling verses and introspective pauses. Raw, analog textures. Emphasis on sincerity, silence, and tension in delivery.

songs.title: World Stage – Rock Day Anthem
songs.tags:
style: Stadium Rock Anthem, inspired by Live Aid. Male lead, expressive and epic. Backing vocals for choir effect. Classic rock band: electric guitars, bass, live drums, stadium piano, crowd ambience. Grand, uplifting, anthemic, energetic.]
[tempo: 116 BPM]
[structure: Verse – Pre-Chorus – Chorus – Bridge – Final Chorus. Includes instrumental solo and crowd singalong.]
[intro: Crowd noise swelling, powerful piano chords, guitar arpeggio building, drums join with energetic beat.]
[sound: Stadium ambience, reverb on vocals, wide stereo image.]
[build-up: Verse vocals start intimate, build with drums and guitar.]
[chorus: All instruments in, soaring vocals, massive choir, crowd clapping.]
[solo: Electric guitar, emotional and melodic, under stadium lights.]
[bridge: Spoken/whispered phrase over quieter instrumental, then explosion to final chorus.]

songs.title: Stereo Guitar Duet
songs.tags:
Instrumental guitar duet—no vocals, no rhythm section, no synths, no background chords or accompaniment.  
Two lead guitars only: Guitar 1 panned left, Guitar 2 panned right.  
Performance: conversational and interactive—trading phrases, harmonizing, call and response, unison riffs, or contrapuntal lines.  
Techniques: melodic runs, bends, slides, vibrato, harmonized leads, fingerstyle, tapping, improvisation, creative phrasing.  
Mood: [choose—expressive, playful, bluesy, soulful, technical, cinematic, etc.].  
Recording: dry or natural room reverb, both guitars fully exposed, no overdubs, stereo field wide.  
Influences: [M ark Knopfler & C het Atkins, E ric Clapton & B B King, R odrigo y G abriela, J immy Page & The Edge, P at Metheny & J ohn Scofield, etc.].  
Avoid: bass, drums, synths, pads, vocals, dense effects, backing tracks, excessive layering. Focus on pure two-guitar interplay in stereo.

songs.title: celebração do início ao fim
songs.tags: Uptempo worship pop/gospel. Female lead: clear, joyful, soaring. Piano, electric guitar, brass, handclaps, drums, choir. Every lyric line starts with English emotional/rhythmic tag. Portuguese lyrics: celebration, praise, joy—jumping, clapping, exalting Jesus. Communal, energetic, full of light. No melancholy, no slow ballad, no electronic FX—pure exuberant worship.

songs.title: intro culture
songs.tags: High-energy electronic dance music track characterized by a driving four-on-the-floor kick drum and a prominent, syncopated synth bassline. The arrangement features a bright, rhythmic synth lead that plays a repetitive, catchy hook. The tempo is approximately 128 BPM in the key of G minor. The production is polished with heavy sidechain compression on the pads and bass to create a pumping effect. The structure includes a build-up with rising white noise sweeps and a snare roll, leading into a high-impact drop where the main synth melody and heavy kick dominate. Vocal elements consist of processed, rhythmic vocal chops and short, energetic phrases that are heavily EQ'd and layered with reverb. The mix is wide with crisp high-end percussion and a solid, centered low-end.

songs.title: That's How Much I Loved You
songs.tags: Powerful emotional rock ballad with raspy male vocal in the style of Bryan Adam. Tempo 70 BPM, acoustic-driven with subtle piano and clean guitar. Deep emotional delivery, soft verses building into strong choruses with expressive vocal rasp and genuine vulnerability. Natural breaths, emotional pauses, intimate vocal presence. Cinematic structure, warm analog textures, nostalgic tone. Ideal for heartfelt, introspective storytelling, emphasizing authentic emotion and vocal honesty.

songs.title: É Tudo Sobre Você
songs.tags:
Modern Brazilian worship ballad (G abriela Rocha style): 
expressive, honest female vocal; piano, acoustic/electric guitar, strings, pads, drums. Portuguese lyrics: repetitive, direct, total surrender, focus on God alone—collective, pure praise. Each line starts with English emotion/rhythm tag. No autotune, no pop, no choir, no R&B, no show-off—only humble, sincere worship.

songs.title: No Mercy, No Return
songs.tags: Duet: Male, harsh, shouted, rapid-fire (Tom Araya) + male, demonic baritone, spoken/growled. Vocals trade, overlap, echo, and taunt, creating a hellish, apocalyptic atmosphere. Lyrics: war, blood, pain, chaos, death, reign, hell, darkness—no mercy. Instrumentation: hyperfast, downpicked guitars, wild solos, pounding double-kick, analog mix. Mood: violent, infernal, relentless. Influences: Slayer (Raining Blood, South of Heaven). NO autotune, NO polish, NO harmonies—just raw, demonic thrash.

songs.title: Shadows Never Sleep
songs.tags: Lead vocal in the style of O zzy O sbourne at his peak (early B lack Sabbath and early solo era). Male tenor voice is powerful, piercing, and resonant—slightly nasal but with remarkable clarity and wide, haunting vibrato. Phrasing is dramatic and stretched, alternating between melodic singing and half-spoken lines. Signature Ozzy cries (“Oh Lord, yeah!”) are delivered with full chest and emotional bite. Vocals cut through heavy guitars, melodic bass, and live drums, often riding above the mix with analog warmth. Lyrics evoke darkness, existential longing, and ghostly memories. Arrangement is mid-tempo, live and raw, as if recorded onstage in the 1970s. Everything channels O zzy’s iconic, untamed energy and supernatural charisma at his peak.

songs.title: Eu Fui Chamado — A Voz de Paulo | LineScript v6
songs.tags:
[STYLE / CONTEXT:]
[Cinematic worship ballad — biblical storytelling in first person.
Based on the Apostle Paul’s conversion and calling (Atos 9).
Designed as a companion piece to “Sacrifícios”, showing transformation and mission.
Acoustic + orchestral texture with ambient depth — introspective yet powerful.]

[VOICE PERSONA — MALE LEAD:]
[Register: baritone to high tenor (A2–E4)]
[Timbre: earthy (7), emotional (9), slightly raspy (5), warm (8), ancient-soul resonance (10)]
[Dynamics: wide — whispers on verses, outcry on chorus peaks]
[Breath: audible in transitions; symbol of spiritual struggle]
[Vibrato: natural and slow (4)]
[Attack: soft onset, firm consonant emphasis in testimony phrases]
[Unique traits: cracked tone at emotional words (“graça”, “cruz”, “estrada”), sighs before refrains, trembling sustain at the end of “nada”.]
[Persona imagery: Paulo idoso, em cela fria, relembrando sua conversão com fé inabalável.]

songs.title: Born to Ride, Never Grow Old
songs.tags: Male vocal—raspy, warm, direct, soulful, inspired by J ohn Fogerty (Creedence C learwater Revival). Roots rock, swamp rock, Southern blues vibe. Clean and crunchy guitars, bass, live drums, organ/piano fills, harmonica, occasional acoustic. Band-in-the-room analog mix—dry, raw, open. Lyrics tell stories of the road, nature, hope, working class, and freedom. NO autotune, synths, pop production, or modern polish—only earthy, live, timeless Americana rock.

songs.tags:
[STYLE: Spiritual Worship Ballad, Intimate Contemporary Worship]
[MOOD: Tenderness, Reverent Surrender, Sacred Hope]
[BPM: 68–72 BPM]
[KEY: G major]

songs.title: The Hollow Truth  (Edit)
songs.tags: A cinematic power ballad rock track at 66 BPM: verses open with wide acoustic guitar (L), cinematic piano (mid-L), mono bass, ambient rear/top strings, and soft rim-clicks. Chorus explodes—distorted guitar (R), full kit, soaring centered lead guitar that pans during solos, thunder FX in bridge. Male vocals shift from broken and intimate to gritty, then scream-falsetto, front and center with deep stereo echoes. Reverb, volume, and spatial FX evolve each section, building an enveloping, cathartic soundscape.

songs.title: Kings of the Runaway
songs.tags:
(Vocal: Male lead, inspired by S teven Tyler. G ritty, expressive, high-energy with dynamic range and trademark slides, growls, and falsetto. Emphasize charisma, attitude, and playful phrasing. Phrasing should be tight and rhythmic, with bursts of emotion and controlled aggression—distinctive “rock star” presence.)

Instrumentation: Classic rock band—electric guitars with crunchy riffs and melodic solos, funky bass lines, punchy live drums with occasional fills, honky-tonk piano or bluesy organ layers as optional. Groove-driven, mid-to-fast tempo, with a loose and swaggering feel. Arrangement should spotlight the interplay between vocals and lead guitar, with bold hooks and memorable choruses. Production should sound live, energetic, and warm, with clear separation for every instrument.

songs.title: Raul - desafinado
songs.tags:
Title: Carona no Disco Riscado

Vintage Brazilian rock inspired by R aul Seixas, blending philosophical spoken word and bluesy groove. Features baritone male narration with irony and existential humor, vintage electric guitar riffs, groovy melodic bass, dry classic drums, and retro organ textures. The arrangement is raw and organic, channeling 70s analog warmth and playful lyricism. A manifesto-style breakdown, delivered in a confessional, poetic tone, questions identity, fate, and the art of singing without a voice. After the breakdown, the song returns to guitar and organ interplay, fading out like an old radio memory. The mood is existential, irreverent, a bit psychedelic, perfect for projects seeking a blend of rock authenticity and Brazilian poetic soul, echoing Raul’s tradition of turning every song into both road and journey.

songs.title: When We Touch The Light — Euphoric Deep Tech Fusion v5 Serene
songs.tags:
This high-energy minimal techno track unfolds at 129 BPM with a sparse piano intro atop an airy pad, delivering serene, melancholy warmth. Distant strings glide into the verse, joined by brushed drums and muted electric guitar for gentle motion. The chorus rises with lush, glowing synths and a haunting, melodic countermelody, underpinned by polyrhythmic arpeggios and syncopated ghost hats. The analog-digital fusion creates luminous clarity and emotional depth; Mixolydian shifts in the drops infuse the dancefloor with liberating euphoria while maintaining textural richness and flow.

[STYLE / CONTEXT:]
[High-Energy Minimal Techno with melodic euphoria.]
[Inspired by Stephan Bodzin, Adriatique, Colyn — a balance between cinematic joy and dancefloor power.]
[Designed for immersive live club experiences, with a glowing analog-digital warmth.]

[MOOD:]
[Euphoric, luminous, hypnotic, emotional, liberating.]
[Feels like awakening under strobe lights — the sound of freedom and reunion.]

songs.title: Harmony Ocean Front
� CAPÍTULO 14 — PROMPT 03: WEAPON D-011 (The Closer / Deconstruction)
songs.tags: Minimalist techno focused on sub-pressure overdrive and relentless kinetic thrust. Weapon D-011 v1.5 integrates the **SPES CHIRP Earprint** to establish immediate sonic identity. The track utilizes a **Closer Engine** structure, quickly building to its peak before executing a long, filter-driven progressive dissolution. Energy is continuous, relying on density and controlled transient sharpening rather than FX. Breaks are short, utilizing silence pockets. Designed as a professional set closer, emphasizing stability, constant pressure, and clean, elegant fade-out.

songs.title: First Light in the Garage
songs.tags: Acoustic ballad blending the warmth and patience of 80s classic rock with the smoky, poetic vocal style of Jim Morrison (The Doors). Features fingerpicked acoustic guitar, natural string and fret noise, expressive whistling melody, and sparse vintage percussion (brushes, shaker, tambourine). Male baritone vocals are intimate, dynamic, and often mix singing and spoken word. Arrangement is minimal, analog, and nostalgic, capturing the sound of a late-night live take in an old studio. Every instrument and vocal is given space to breathe. The mood is world-weary but hopeful, evoking longing, memory, and quiet reflection.

songs.title: Smile Like a Cadillac
songs.tags: Male vocal, low, gritty, bluesy, playful—modeled after Billy Gibbons (Z Z Top). Swagger, sly humor, cool in verses, bold in choruses, signature vocal slides/drawl. Lyrics about cars, Texas, boots, parties, bars, women, highways, groove—using words like car, engine, highway, queen, hat, radio, wild, whiskey, dance. Instrumentation: fuzzy/distorted guitar, slide, walking bass, swinging live drums, harmonica, cowbell, synth stabs. Mood: fun, confident, Texas-proud, mischievous, carefree. Influences: Z Z Top (La Grange, Sharp Dressed Man). NO autotune, NO pop, NO modern layering—just raw, analog, blues-driven boogie.

[Attack: soft onset, firm consonant emphasis in testimony phrases]
[Unique traits: cracked tone at emotional words (“graça”, “cruz”, “estrada”), sighs before refrains, trembling sustain at the end of “nada”.]
[Persona imagery: Paulo idoso, em cela fria, relembrando sua conversão com fé inabalável.]

songs.title: Menino da Porteira – Última Cavalgada (Versão Continuação Caipira)
Fé Pra Virar o Jogo
songs.tags: Brazilian gospel ballad, L udmila Ferber style: prayerful, soft, gentle female vocal, minimal vibrato, intimate delivery. Instrumentation: piano, acoustic guitar, soft strings, pad, gentle percussion. Lyrics are short, repetitive, mantra-like; Portuguese, message of faith, hope, and resilience in adversity. No autotune, no pop or R&B stylings, no gospel choir, no fast tempo, no dramatic arrangements—only sincere, spiritual comfort.

songs.title: Wild Pulse Hyperdrive – Enhanced Bass
songs.tags:
[STYLE: Hi-Tech Psy / Psycore / Tribal Tech]
    [MOOD: Intense, Hypnotic, Primal, Elevated]
    [VOCAL STYLE: Sporadic tribal shouts; processed animal calls]
    [INSTRUMENTATION: (Ultra heavy sub kick) (Bass: ultra fat sustained bass-guitar synth layer locked to kick) (Distorted rolling bass) (Resonant psy synth stabs) (Human whistles and bird whistles) (Gated heartbeats) (Hand claps with reverse reverb) (Dog barks and wolf howls) (Owl hoots and insect chirps) (Elephant calls with convolution reverb) (Glitch percussion grains and tribal toms) (Engine rev loops and mechanical hum) (Animal roars and galloping hoof loops)]
    [BPM: 172]
    [TIME_SIGNATURE: 4/4 with embedded polyrhythms]

songs.title: Reign in Blood
songs.tags: Male vocal, harsh, aggressive, rapid-fire—modeled after Tom Araya (Slayer). Shouted, piercing, dark, full of rage and urgency. Lyrics about war, death, blood, violence, apocalypse, chaos—words like blood, war, pain, reign, scream, hell, blade, ashes. Instrumentation: hyper-fast, downtuned, palm-muted guitars, frenzied solos, pounding double-kick drums, relentless bass, dry analog mix, minimal effects. Mood: violent, chaotic, critical, dark, “no escape.” Influences: Slayer (Raining Blood, Angel of Death). NO autotune, NO pop, NO polish—just pure analog thrash terror.

songs.title: Out on the Line -  (Jon Bon Jovi)
songs.tags: Male vocal—bold, raspy, anthemic, passionate. Inspired by Jon B on Jovi, energy of classic stadium rock, soaring choruses, guitar-driven sound, emotional storytelling. Lyrics about the road, fire, hope, pain, memory, rising against fate, promises kept. Instrumentation: layered electric/acoustic guitars, punchy live drums, melodic bass, epic solos, crowd harmonies, cinematic pads. Mood: triumphant, hopeful, bittersweet, epic. Influences: B on Jovi, B ryan Adams, D ef Leppard. NO EDM, autotune, trap/urban beats, or minimal pop elements—just pure arena rock spirit.

songs.title: Lua e Flor (Poetic Acoustic Tribute)
songs.tags: Solo male baritone voice, gentle, poetic, with intimate vibrato and storytelling. Nylon-string acoustic guitar, fingerstyle and arpeggios, plenty of silence and breath between lines. Every phrase is short, evocative, and guided by emotional instructions. No percussion, bass, synths, or FX. Preserves introspection and lyrical space—true to Oswaldo Montenegro’s acoustic artistry.

songs.title: Inspirado em Jon Bon Jovi – stadium rock era, c.1986–1992
Infinito
songs.tags: A Latin Pop and Romantic Acoustic Reggaeton track at 89 BPM, led by nylon guitar with intricate fingerstyle patterns, mellow reggaeton dembow, deep saturated sub-bass, and Latin percussion accents. Ambient pads, analog synth swells, and Rhodes with tremolo add emotional layers. Ocean ambience, vinyl crackle, and earthen FX textures frame the intro and outro, while soft percussive rimshots, bongos, pandeiro, and melodic fills glide throughout to evoke a lush, cinematic poetic pop sound.

songs.title: Sou Teu Milagre
songs.tags: Modern worship pop ballad, Lauren Dai gle style: rich, soulful, expressive female vocal; warm timbre, confessional delivery, subtle rasp. Piano, pads, acoustic/electric guitar, drums, strings. Portuguese lyrics: honest, devotional, faith, identity, belonging. Each line with English emotion/rhythm tag. No autotune, no pop runs, no gospel choir, no electronic beats—only soulful, pure worship.
